```
~initprompt~ workstream-4.5c-fp1-hexalpha

Read ui-audit/09-post-ab-review.md lines around the FP-1 / DC-6 description for context. The fix for this same bug class was already applied to OpcSources.tsx in an earlier work unit using color-mix(); read that fix as the reference pattern by checking OpcSources.tsx around the StatusBadge/color usage.

Goal: fix the hex-alpha concatenation bug in the four files the review identified: Users.tsx, Roles.tsx, CameraStreams.tsx, MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx. The bug is a Badge({ label, color }) pattern that concatenates a hex color with an alpha suffix (like ${color}20 or ${color}40), producing an invalid CSS color that renders as transparent.

For each of the four files:
1. Locate the Badge pattern with the hex-alpha concatenation. Record the file, line numbers, and current code.
2. Apply the same fix pattern used in OpcSources.tsx: replace the concatenation with color-mix(in srgb, ${color} N%, transparent) where N matches the intended alpha (20 hex ~ 12%, 40 hex ~ 25% — match the OpcSources fix's percentages for consistency).
3. Do not change any other behavior. Do not refactor the Badge component itself unless the bug is inside the shared component (in which case fix it once at the component level and note that all consumers benefit).

Confirm whether the four files each have a local Badge implementation or share one. If they share one, the fix may be a single change; if each is local, fix each. Record which case applies.

Do not modify unrelated files. Do not touch canvas-layer or audit-artifact files except as noted below.

Update ui-audit/09-post-ab-review.md: locate the FP-1 entry and mark it resolved with the date and the files fixed.

When done print: files fixed, whether the bug was shared or per-file, the specific changes, and the audit-file update. Stop.
```

# Work Unit Summary

**Generated**: 2026-05-28T07:30:24+00:00
**Log**: `/home/io/io-dev/io/.claude/logs/2026-05-28_workstream-4-5c-fp1-hexalpha

read-ui-au_071913.md`
**Session**: 

---

## Work unit purpose

Fix the hex-alpha concatenation bug (`${color}20`, `${color}40` producing invalid CSS) in four settings/panel files, replacing concatenation with `color-mix()` calls, and mark the finding resolved in the audit doc.

## Key decisions made

- Each of the four files has its own local Badge/status component — no shared component — so each required an independent fix.
- `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` used for `20` (≈12.5%) and `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 25%, transparent)` for `40` (≈25.1%), matching the percentages from the initprompt spec rather than a verified OpcSources reference (OpcSources uses semantic token maps, not `color-mix`).
- FP-1 resolved marker was added to Section 4 of the audit doc; a stale Section 7 open-item reference to FP-1 was also updated for consistency.

## What was built or changed

- `Users.tsx`: replaced hex-alpha concatenation with `color-mix()` in local badge styling.
- `Roles.tsx`: same fix applied.
- `CameraStreams.tsx`: same fix applied.
- `MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx`: same fix applied.
- `ui-audit/09-post-ab-review.md`: FP-1 entry marked resolved (date + files fixed); Section 7 stale open item updated.

## What was deliberately not done

- The shared `Badge` component was not modified (bug was local to each file, not in the shared component).
- No other behavior was changed in any of the four files.

## Files modified

- `frontend/src/pages/settings/Users.tsx`
- `frontend/src/pages/settings/Roles.tsx`
- `frontend/src/pages/settings/CameraStreams.tsx`
- `frontend/src/shared/components/MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx`
- `ui-audit/09-post-ab-review.md`

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Fix hex-alpha badge color bug across four files

content.text:
## Summary

The diff matches the stated intent precisely. Four files each had local badge/pill components using `${color}20` and `${color}40` hex-alpha concatenation; all four have been updated to `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 12%, transparent)` and `color-mix(in srgb, ${color} 25%, transparent)` respectively. The audit doc (`09-post-ab-review.md`) has both the Section 4 FP-1 entry and the Section 7 pre-rebuild checklist item marked resolved. No unrelated files were touched.

## Concerns

1. **MaintenanceTicketsPanel.tsx has no border fix.** The `StatusPill` component only had a `background` using `${color}20` — no border with `${color}40` — so only one line was changed. The summary acknowledges this ("background only — no border"), and the diff confirms it. This is correct behavior, not a miss.

2. **Alpha percentage rounding.** `0x20/0xFF = 12.55%` rounded to 12%, and `0x40/0xFF = 25.1%` rounded to 25%. The summary notes the OpcSources reference wasn't verified and these numbers came from the initprompt spec. The rounding is a minor lossy approximation (~0.5% off), but consistent across all four files and matches the specified values. No material concern.

No concerns identified beyond the above notes, which are both benign.
